title: Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Jharkhand

overview.short: Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Jharkhand, India, about 55 miles (89 km) north of Ranchi.
overview.description: Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Jharkhand, India, about 55 miles (89 km) north of Ranchi. It was established in 1955. It is home to many wild animals including deer, monkey and birds.

topPlaces.name: Padma, Hazaribagh
topPlaces.description: Padma is a village in the Padma CD block in the Barhi subdivision of the Hazaribagh district in the Indian state of Jharkhand.> Padma is historically known for the Padma Fort (also called Padma Kila or Hawamahal), which served as the seat of the Ramgarh Raj family during the British period. faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gaya International Airport (78 km) and the nearest railway station is Gaya Junction (81 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Padma, Hazaribagh (community development block), Padma, Hazaribagh, Ichak, Ichak (community development block).
